# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks across a network of workers. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and efficient, allowing for the execution of a wide range of tasks with varying requirements.

## Components

### Task

A task is a unit of work that needs to be executed. It is defined by a set of parameters, including the task type, input data, and any constraints on the execution of the task.

### Worker

A worker is a service that can execute tasks. Workers are responsible for performing the actual work required by the task, and they are selected by the task execution engine based on their capabilities and availability.

### Task Execution Engine

# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks by multiple workers, with the goal of producing high-quality outputs while adhering to constraints on cost, time, and other factors. The system is designed to be flexible, allowing for the use of different workers with different capabilities and costs, and to be extensible, allowing for the addition of new workers and new types of tasks.

## Key Components

### Task

A task is a unit of work that needs to be performed. It is defined by a set of inputs, a set of outputs, and a set of constraints on the execution of the task. The inputs and outputs are typically represented as JSON objects, and the constraints are represented as a set of key-value pairs.

### Worker

A worker is an entity that can perform tasks.

```
# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that uses a set of workers to perform tasks. It is designed to be flexible and extensible, allowing for a wide range of use cases. It is also designed to be easy to use, with a simple API that can be used to submit tasks and retrieve results.

## Components

Dot Loom is composed of the following components:

- **Task**: A task is a unit of work that is to be performed by a worker. It is defined by a set of inputs and a set of outputs.
- **Worker**: A worker is a service that performs tasks. It is defined by a set of capabilities and a set of constraints.
```

# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks by a set of workers. It is designed to be highly configurable and extensible, allowing for the creation of complex workflows that can be tailored to specific needs.

## Components

### Task

A task is a unit of work that is to be executed by a worker. It is defined by a set of parameters that describe the work to be done, such as the input data, the expected output, and the constraints on the execution.

### Worker

# Dot Loom

## Overview

Dot Loom is a new execution engine for Dot. It is designed to be a more efficient and scalable way to run Dot tasks. It is also designed to be more flexible and to support a wider range of use cases.

## Goals

- **Efficiency**: Dot Loom should be more efficient than the current execution engine. It should be able to run tasks faster and with less overhead.
- **Scalability**: Dot Loom should be able to scale to handle a large number of tasks. It should be able to handle tasks of varying complexity and size.
- **Flexibility**: Dot Loom should be flexible enough to support a wide range of use cases. It should be able to handle tasks that require different levels of quality, speed, and cost

+++++ docs/design/2024-01-01-dot-loom-architecture.md
# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks across a network of workers. It is designed to be highly configurable, allowing users to specify the desired outcome of a task, the constraints under which it should be executed, and the workers that are available to perform the task.

## Components

Dot Loom consists of the following components:

- **Task**: A task is a unit of work that Dot Loom is responsible for executing. It is defined by a set of inputs, a desired outcome, and a set of constraints.
- **Worker**: A worker is a service that is capable of executing tasks. Workers are registered with Dot Loom and are assigned tasks based on their capabilities and availability.

```
# LLM Agents

## Overview

This document describes the design of LLM agents, which are autonomous AI systems that can perform tasks and make decisions based on their own reasoning and knowledge. LLM agents are powered by large language models (LLMs), which are neural networks that can generate human-like text based on input prompts.

## Motivation

LLM agents have the potential to revolutionize many aspects of human life, from education and entertainment to healthcare and business. They can provide personalized and interactive experiences, assist with complex and creative tasks, and learn from feedback and interaction. However, LLM agents also pose significant challenges and risks, such as ethical, legal, and social implications, as well as technical limitations and uncertainties.
```

```
# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks by a set of workers. It is designed to be highly configurable and extensible, allowing for a wide range of use cases.

## Components

### Task

A task is a unit of work that needs to be executed. It is defined by a set of parameters, including the task type, input data, and output data.

### Worker

A worker is a service that can execute tasks. It is defined by a set of parameters, including the worker type, capabilities, and availability.

### Orchestrator

The orchestrator is the core component of Dot Loom. It is responsible for managing the execution of tasks by workers. It is designed
```

# Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of large language models (LLMs) to perform complex tasks. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and easy to use. It is built on top of the [LangChain](https://www.langchain.com/) framework, which provides a set of tools for working with LLMs.

## Architecture

Dot Loom is composed of the following components:

- **Dot Loom API**: A RESTful API that provides a simple interface for interacting with the system. It is built on top of the [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/) framework.
- **Dot Loom Core**: The core of the system, which is responsible for orchestrating the execution of LLMs. It is built on top of the [LangChain](https

```
# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks across a network of workers. It is designed to be highly flexible, allowing for the execution of a wide variety of tasks, from simple data processing to complex decision-making.

## Components

Dot Loom is composed of several key components:

- **Task Manager**: The Task Manager is responsible for managing the execution of tasks. It receives tasks from clients, assigns them to workers, and monitors their progress.
- **Worker Manager**: The Worker Manager is responsible for managing the workers in the network. It maintains a pool of available workers, assigns tasks to them, and monitors their performance.
- **Task Executor**: The Task Executor is responsible for executing tasks
```

```
# Design

## Overview

The Dot Loom system is designed to be a flexible and efficient tool for managing and executing complex tasks. It is built on a modular architecture that allows for easy integration of new components and features. The system is designed to be scalable, allowing it to handle a large number of tasks and users.

## Components

The Dot Loom system is composed of several key components:

- **Task Manager**: The Task Manager is responsible for managing the lifecycle of tasks. It receives tasks from users, assigns them to workers, and monitors their progress. The Task Manager is also responsible for handling task dependencies and ensuring that tasks are executed in the correct order.

- **Worker Manager**: The Worker Manager is responsible for managing the workers that execute tasks. It assigns tasks to workers, monitors their progress, and handles worker failures. The Worker Manager is
```

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks using a set of workers. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and efficient, and to provide a high level of control over the execution of tasks.

## Key Concepts

### Task

A task is a unit of work that needs to be executed. It is defined by a set of parameters, including the task type, the input data, and the desired output. Tasks can be simple or complex, and can be executed by one or more workers.

### Worker

A worker is a service that can execute tasks. Workers are defined by a set of parameters, including the worker type, the input data, and the desired output. Workers can be simple or complex, and can be executed by one or more tasks.

### Execution Plan

```

The output is a JSON object with the following fields:

- `access`: A map from role to worker ID. The `writer` is the worker that will write the final answer. The `reviewer` is the worker that will review the final answer. The `finalizer` is the worker that will finalize the final answer. If a role is not present, it means that no worker is assigned to that role.
- `estimated_credits`: The estimated number of credits that will be used by the execution plan.
- `estimated_latency_ms`: The estimated latency in milliseconds of the execution plan.
- `estimated_pass_rate`: The estimated pass rate of the execution plan.
- `estimated_quality`: The estimated quality of the execution plan.
- `finalizer`: The worker ID of the finalizer, or `null` if no finalizer is assigned.
```

# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks by multiple workers, each with different capabilities and costs. The engine is designed to be flexible, allowing for the addition of new workers and the modification of existing ones. It is also designed to be scalable, allowing for the execution of tasks in parallel.

## Components

### Task

A task is a unit of work that is to be executed by one or more workers. A task is defined by a set of inputs, a set of outputs, and a set of constraints. The inputs are the data that the task requires to be executed. The outputs are the data that the task produces. The constraints are the conditions that the task must satisfy.

### Worker

```
# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks by multiple AI workers. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and efficient, while also being easy to use and understand.

## Key Concepts

### Task

A task is a unit of work that is to be performed by one or more AI workers. It is defined by a set of inputs, outputs, and constraints.

### Worker

A worker is an AI model that is capable of performing tasks. It is defined by a set of capabilities, constraints, and costs.

### Orchestrator

The orchestrator is the component of Dot Loom that is responsible for managing the execution of tasks by workers. It is responsible for selecting the appropriate workers for each task, scheduling the execution of tasks, and monitoring the progress of tasks
```

# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks by a set of workers. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and efficient, and to support a wide range of use cases.

## Components

Dot Loom is composed of the following components:

- **Task**: A task is a unit of work that is to be executed by a worker. It is defined by a set of inputs, outputs, and constraints.
- **Worker**: A worker is an entity that can execute tasks. It is defined by a set of capabilities, constraints, and costs.
- **Execution Plan**: An execution plan is a set of instructions that define how a task is to be executed by a set of workers. It is

```
# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks using a set of workers. The system is designed to be flexible, allowing for the use of different workers with different capabilities and costs. The system is also designed to be efficient, allowing for the execution of tasks in parallel and the use of caching to avoid redundant work.

## Components

- **Task**: A task is a unit of work that needs to be executed. A task has a set of inputs, a set of outputs, and a set of constraints. The inputs and outputs are represented as JSON objects, and the constraints are represented as a set of key-value pairs.
```

```
# Dot Loom

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks by multiple workers. It is designed to be flexible, efficient, and scalable, and to support a wide range of use cases.

## Key Concepts

### Workers

Workers are the entities that perform tasks. They can be of different types, such as human workers, AI models, or other software agents. Workers have different capabilities, costs, and performance characteristics.

### Tasks

Tasks are the units of work that workers perform. They can be of different types, such as text generation, image classification, or code review. Tasks have different requirements, such as input data, output format, and quality standards.
```

# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks using a set of workers. The system is designed to be flexible, allowing for the use of different workers with different capabilities and costs. The system is also designed to be efficient, allowing for the execution of tasks in parallel and the use of caching to reduce the number of calls to workers.

- **Task**: A task is a unit of work that needs to be executed. A task is defined by a set of inputs and a set of outputs. The inputs are the data that the task needs to process, and the outputs are the data that the task produces.

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that uses a set of workers to complete tasks. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and efficient. It is also designed to be easy to use and to integrate with other systems.

### Workers

Workers are the entities that perform the actual work. They are responsible for completing tasks and returning results. # Design

## Overview

The Dot Loom system is designed to be a flexible and efficient tool for managing complex tasks. It is built on a set of core components that work together to provide a robust and scalable solution.

## Core Components

### Task

The `Task` component is the central unit of work in the Dot Loom system. It represents a specific job that needs to be completed, such as writing an article, generating a report, or performing a data analysis. Each `Task` has a set of attributes that define its characteristics, such as the category, complexity, and consequence of the task.

# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks by multiple workers, with the goal of maximizing the utility of the output. The system is designed to be flexible, allowing for a wide range of tasks and workers, and to be extensible, allowing for new workers and tasks to be added easily.

## Key Concepts

### Task

A task is a unit of work that is to be performed by one or more workers. A task is defined by a set of inputs, a set of outputs, and a set of constraints. The inputs are the data that the worker will use to perform the task. The outputs are the data that the worker will produce as a result of performing the task. The constraints are the limits on the resources that the worker can use to perform the task.

```
# Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a framework for orchestrating large language models (LLMs) to perform complex tasks. It is designed to be flexible, extensible, and easy to use. It is built on top of the [LangChain](https://github.com/hwchase17/langchain) framework, which provides a set of tools for working with LLMs.

## Architecture

Dot Loom is composed of the following components:

- **Dot Loom**: The main orchestration engine. It is responsible for managing the execution of tasks, coordinating the work of workers, and ensuring that the task is completed successfully.
- **Workers**: The workers are the individual LLMs that are used to perform the tasks. They are responsible for executing the individual steps of the task.
```

```
# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks by multiple AI workers. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and efficient, while also being easy to use and understand.

## Key Concepts

### Task

A task is a unit of work that is to be performed by one or more AI workers. It is defined by a set of inputs, a set of outputs, and a set of constraints.

### Worker

A worker is an AI model that is capable of performing tasks. It is defined by a set of capabilities, a set of constraints, and a set of costs.

### Orchestrator

The orchestrator is the component of Dot Loom that is responsible for managing the execution of tasks by workers. It is responsible for selecting the appropriate workers for each task,
```

# Design

## Overview

The Dot Loom system is designed to be a flexible and efficient tool for managing and executing complex tasks. It is built on a modular architecture that allows for easy integration of new components and features. The system is designed to be scalable, allowing it to handle a large number of tasks and users.

## Components

The Dot Loom system is composed of several key components:

- **Task Manager**: The Task Manager is responsible for managing the lifecycle of tasks. It receives tasks from users, assigns them to workers, and monitors their progress. # Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks using a set of workers. The system is designed to be flexible, allowing for the use of different workers with different capabilities and costs. The system is also designed to be scalable, allowing for the execution of tasks in parallel.

## Components

The Dot Loom system consists of the following components:

- **Task**: A task is a unit of work that needs to be executed. A task is defined by a set of inputs, a set of outputs, and a set of constraints.

# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks using a set of workers. The system is designed to be flexible, allowing for the use of different workers with different capabilities and costs. The system is also designed to be efficient, allowing for the execution of tasks in parallel and the use of caching to avoid redundant work.

## Components

The Dot Loom system consists of the following components:

- **Dot Loom**: The main component of the system, responsible for orchestrating the execution of tasks.
- **Workers**: The workers are the components that perform the actual work. They are responsible for executing tasks and returning results.

# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks using a set of workers. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and efficient, allowing for the execution of complex tasks with minimal overhead.

## Components

### Task

A task is a unit of work that needs to be executed. It is defined by a set of parameters, including the task type, input data, and output requirements. Tasks are executed by workers, which are responsible for performing the actual work.

### Worker

A worker is a service that performs a specific type of work. Workers are responsible for executing tasks and returning the results. Workers can be of different types, such as a language model, a code interpreter, or a data processing service

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of AI agents. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and easy to use. It is built on top of the [Dot Loom API](https://github.com/dotloom/dotloom-api), which provides a simple and consistent interface for interacting with AI agents.

- **Dot Loom API**: A RESTful API that provides a simple and consistent interface for interacting with AI agents.
- **Dot Loom CLI**: A command-line interface for interacting with the Dot Loom API.
- **Dot Loom Web**: A web interface for interacting with the Dot Loom API.

# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks across a network of workers. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and efficient, allowing for the execution of a wide range of tasks with varying requirements.

## Components

### Task

A task is a unit of work that needs to be executed. It is defined by a set of parameters, including the task type, input data, and any constraints on the execution of the task.

### Worker

A worker is a node in the network that is capable of executing tasks. Workers are responsible for receiving tasks, executing them, and returning the results. Workers can be of different types, each with its own capabilities and constraints.

The Dot Loom system is designed to be a flexible and efficient tool for managing and executing complex tasks using a variety of AI models. It is built on the principles of modularity, scalability, and adaptability, allowing it to handle a wide range of tasks with varying levels of complexity and resource requirements.

# Dot Loom Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks using a set of workers. The system is designed to be flexible, allowing for the use of different workers with different capabilities and costs. The system is also designed to be scalable, allowing for the execution of tasks in parallel.

# Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks by a set of workers. It is designed to be flexible, extensible, and easy to use. It is also designed to be secure, reliable, and efficient.

## Architecture

Dot Loom is a distributed system that is composed of the following components:

- **Dot Loom API**: The Dot Loom API is a RESTful API that is used to interact with the Dot Loom system. It is used to submit tasks, retrieve results, and manage the system.
- **Dot Loom Engine**: The Dot Loom Engine is the core of the Dot Loom system. It is responsible for orchestrating the execution of tasks by a set of workers. It is also responsible for managing the state of the system.

```
# Design

## Overview

The Dot Loom system is designed to be a flexible and efficient tool for managing and executing complex tasks. It is built on a modular architecture that allows for easy integration of new components and features. The system is designed to be scalable, allowing it to handle a large number of tasks and users.

## Components

The Dot Loom system is composed of several key components:

- **Task Manager**: The Task Manager is responsible for managing the lifecycle of tasks. It receives tasks from users, assigns them to workers, and monitors their progress. The Task Manager is also responsible for handling task dependencies and ensuring that tasks are executed in the correct order.

- **Worker Manager**: The Worker Manager is responsible for managing the workers that execute tasks. It assigns tasks to workers, monitors their progress, and handles worker failures. The Worker Manager is
```

# Design

## Overview

The Dot Loom system is designed to be a flexible and efficient tool for managing and executing complex tasks. It consists of several key components that work together to ensure that tasks are completed accurately and efficiently.

## Components

### Task

The `Task` component represents a single task that needs to be completed. It includes the following properties:

- `id`: A unique identifier for the task.
- `description`: A brief description of the task.
- `input`: The input data required to complete the task.
- `output`: The expected output of the task.
- `status`: The current status of the task (e.g., pending, in progress, completed).
- `created_at`: The timestamp when the task was created.
- `updated_at`: The timestamp when the task was last updated.

# Dot Loom Architecture

## Overview

Dot Loom is a task execution engine that orchestrates the execution of tasks across a network of workers. It is designed to be highly configurable, allowing users to specify the desired outcome of a task, the constraints under which it should be executed, and the workers that are available to perform the task.

## Components

Dot Loom consists of the following components:

- **Task**: A task is a unit of work that Dot Loom is responsible for executing. It is defined by a set of inputs, a desired outcome, and a set of constraints.
- **Worker**: A worker is a service that is capable of performing a task. Workers are registered with Dot Loom and are assigned tasks based on their capabilities and availability.

# Design

## Overview

The Dot Loom system is designed to be a flexible and efficient tool for orchestrating the execution of tasks using a variety of workers. It is built on the principles of modularity, scalability, and adaptability, allowing it to handle a wide range of tasks and workers with ease.

## Key Components

### Task

A task is the basic unit of work in the Dot Loom system. It is defined by a set of attributes that describe the task, such as its category, complexity, and consequence. Tasks are created by the user and are executed by the system.

### Worker

A worker is an entity that can perform tasks. Workers are defined by a set of attributes that describe their capabilities, such as their strengths, quality, and pass rate. Workers are provided by the user and are selected by the system

# Design

## Overview

Dot Loom is a system for orchestrating the execution of tasks by a set of workers. It is designed to be flexible, scalable, and easy to use. It is built on top of the [Dot Loom API](https://github.com/dotloom/dotloom-api) and the [Dot Loom SDK](https://github.com/dotloom/dotloom-sdk).

## Architecture

Dot Loom is a distributed system that is composed of the following components:

- **Dot Loom API**: The API is the entry point for all interactions with the Dot Loom system. It is responsible for accepting tasks, managing workers, and orchestrating the execution of tasks.
- **Dot Loom SDK**: The SDK is a library that provides a simple interface for interacting with the Dot Loom

The Dot Loom conductor is a critical component of the Dot Loom system, responsible for selecting the highest-utility execution plan that adheres to all hard call, credit, and latency limits. It uses cheap workers when sufficient and requires an independent provider when consequences and verification needs justify it. The conductor returns exactly one compact JSON object and no prose.

The input to the conductor is a JSON object that includes constraints, task details, and worker information. The constraints specify the maximum number of calls, credits, and latency, as well as the minimum quality required. The task details include the ambiguity, category, complexity, consequence, evidence need, family, input tokens, reversibility, risk, and summary. The worker information includes the credits per call, ID, p95 latency, pass rate, provider group, quality, and strengths.
